response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack web developer with strong software craftsmanship skills, but he fails to meet several hard requirements for this role. Most critically, he has no Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — a non-negotiable requirement for this position. He also lacks any networking or security domain experience (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, network switches, embedded firmware), and while he has touched C/C++ in a limited capacity, his career is predominantly TypeScript/Node.js and web-oriented. The gap between his background and the job's core technical domain is too wide to bridge with general engineering talent alone.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, and embedded contexts demonstrates adaptability and engineering maturity
Hands-on C and C++ exposure at National Instruments and in academic projects (wave field synthesis) shows some low-level systems familiarity
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) indicate disciplined engineering habits that transfer across domains
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — this is a stated mandatory requirement and an immediate disqualifier
Zero experience in networking security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, network switches, Layer 2/3 protocols) that form the core of this role
C programming is peripheral to his career; the overwhelming focus is web/TypeScript, with no evidence of Linux kernel work or embedded firmware development
